title: History, Objects, References, and Forensic Inspection · Graph Retrieval
summary: Explain when and why a behavior changed using graph, path, object, author, and content evidence. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a history-forensics evidence packet after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.
Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
Inspect commits, trees, blobs, tags, and object types without checking out or executing repository content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Log Graph Format evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Log Graph Format assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee3; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Log Graph Format has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Log Graph Format precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee3; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git log lists commits in a universal causal order.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence

Inspect the Blame Context trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.

content.text: For Blame Context, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

stimulus.title: Blame Context case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee4; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The author shown by blame necessarily introduced the current bug.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault. Artifact under review: a history-forensics evidence packet.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Blame Context, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Blame Context: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
